evals.prompt: Review the expression-entry test suite and generate a quality report.
evals.expected_output: A report file is written to docs/reviewReport/runs/REVIEW-<date>-expression-entry.md. Only the file path and Summary table are shown in chat. INDEX.md is updated.
expectations: A .md file is created under docs/reviewReport/runs/ — not pasted into chat
docs/reviewReport/INDEX.md contains a new row for this run
The Summary table in chat contains rows for all 13 categories (A through M)
Categories B, C, H show '✅' or '—' (UI only) — not flagged for API patterns
Category M shows '— not applicable' since this is a UI test scope
Every finding includes a check ID (e.g. B4), the file path, a violation snippet, and a fix snippet
No finding is invented — every cited snippet exists verbatim in the reviewed file

evals.prompt: Check if my expression-entry tests actually cover their test cases.
evals.expected_output: Category K section of the report lists each TC ID found as a comment, confirms whether the matching .md exists, and flags any test case .md files in docs/testCases/ that have no corresponding test() block.
expectations: Each // TC-* comment in the spec is resolved to a docs/testCases/ file
Any TC-* comment with no matching file is flagged as K2 P1
Any .md file in docs/testCases/regression/expression-entry/ with no test() block is flagged as K6 P2
Canvas proxy assertions (not.toBeVisible on expressionError) are NOT flagged as K4 violations

evals.prompt: Scan all my Playwright tests for issues.
evals.expected_output: The report scope is 'all'. Every spec file under src/tests/ — including src/tests/api/ — is reviewed. A single run report and an updated INDEX.md are written to disk.
expectations: Report scope field reads 'all'
Files reviewed list includes every .spec.ts found under src/tests/ including API specs
A single run report is written — not one per spec file
The summary table totals are the sum across all reviewed files
For API spec files, categories B, C, H are marked '— not applicable'
For UI spec files, category M is marked '— not applicable'
Category L (Design Principles) is evaluated for all spec files regardless of type

evals.expected_output: A report file is written to docs/reviewReport/runs/REVIEW-<date>-api.md. Only the file path and Summary table are shown in chat. INDEX.md is updated.
expectations: A .md file is created under docs/reviewReport/runs/ — not pasted into chat
docs/reviewReport/INDEX.md contains a new row for this run
The Summary table shows '— not applicable' for categories B, C, and H
Category M (API Test Architecture) is evaluated against the fixture and spec
Category L (Design Principles) is evaluated for all files in scope
For Category K (API): // TC-API-* comments are matched against docs/APITestCases/calculator-core/
If the coverage status comment lists TCs as pending (⏳), those TCs are NOT flagged as K4 violations
Any TC-API-* reference with no matching file in docs/APITestCases/ is flagged as K2 P1
M1 (unnamespaced window global) is flagged if __testCalculator is used instead of a namespaced key
M3 (no CDN timeout) is flagged if waitForFunction has no explicit timeout option
No finding is invented — every cited snippet exists verbatim in the reviewed file
